skill.name: canvas-design
skill.description: Create design philosophies and polished static visual artwork as Markdown, PNG, or PDF files. The skill emphasizes sophisticated composition, minimal text, typography, and local font use.
skill.summary: Create refined poster-like visual designs from a written design philosophy.
skill.author: Azeem-2

security_audit.summary: Most static high-risk alerts are false positives from Apache and SIL Open Font License boilerplate, not executable code. The only confirmed concern is a skill instruction that permits downloading fonts, plus normal output-file creation. No prompt injection, malicious exfiltration, or command execution intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Unbounded External Font Download Instruction
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the assistant to download whatever fonts are needed. This can cause network access to external font sources and should require user approval or use only trusted sources.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction explicitly permits downloading fonts, which is real network behavior. No destination or exfiltration behavior is present, so the concern is supply-chain and privacy exposure rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: License Text Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Repeated weak-cryptography, reconnaissance, and hardcoded-URL alerts occur in Apache and SIL Open Font License text. These files are legal notices and font license files, not executable code paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are standard license boilerplate and contain no executable instructions. The same wording repeats across the font OFL files, explaining the large number of static alerts.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Command Alert Is False Positive
low_findings.description: The external-command alert is caused by Markdown formatting around a directory path. The line asks the assistant to search the local font directory and does not invoke a shell command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious text is a Markdown inline code span, not Ruby or shell execution syntax in a script. There is no command string, interpreter call, or user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: Expected Output File Creation
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to produce Markdown, PDF, and PNG files. This is expected for a design-generation skill, but users should choose an output directory intentionally.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-writing behavior is explicit and aligns with the skill purpose. No evidence found of destructive writes, hidden paths, or unauthorized file modification.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Resource Acquisition Without Source Limits
dangerous_patterns.description: The font-download instruction does not restrict downloads to trusted domains or user-approved assets. This can introduce untrusted files into the workspace.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is clearly requested by the skill text, but no malicious URL or credential handling is present. The risk is contextual and should be mitigated with approval and trusted sources.

content.value_statement: Many visual design prompts produce generic layouts with too much text. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create a design philosophy first, then render polished PNG or PDF artwork.
actual_capabilities: Creates a written design philosophy before producing the visual artifact.
Guides creation of single-page PNG or PDF artwork with minimal text.
Uses local canvas-fonts assets for more varied typography.
Emphasizes composition, spacing, color, hierarchy, and polish.
Supports multi-page visual series when the user requests additional pages.
limitations: It is instruction-only and does not include a rendering engine.
It may suggest downloading fonts unless constrained by the user.
It focuses on static visual artifacts, not interactive applications.
It depends on the host model and tools for image or PDF generation.

use_cases.title: Poster Concept Development
use_cases.description: Turn a theme, event, or reference into a refined poster direction and final static artwork.

use_cases.title: Editorial Visual Art
use_cases.description: Create magazine-style abstract visuals that communicate through form, color, and sparse typography.

use_cases.title: Presentation Feature Image
use_cases.description: Produce a polished single-page visual for a report, talk, workshop, or internal concept deck.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Poster
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a one-page design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use canvas-design to create a one-page PNG poster about [topic]. Keep text minimal and make the design feel polished.

prompt_templates.title: Event Artwork
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt with audience and mood constraints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use canvas-design to create PDF event artwork for [event]. Include a design philosophy, use local fonts, and keep all text inside the canvas.

prompt_templates.title: Abstract Editorial Piece
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for a subtle conceptual reference.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use canvas-design to create a sophisticated editorial visual about [subject]. Make the reference subtle, visual-first, and supported by sparse typography.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Page Series
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for a connected visual sequence.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use canvas-design to create a three-page PDF series for [theme]. Each page should share one design philosophy but use a distinct composition.

output_examples.input: Create a poster for a small architecture lecture about urban memory.
output: A short design philosophy describing spatial memory, measured rhythm, and restrained typography.
A single-page PDF poster with abstract map-like marks, quiet labels, and strong margins.

output_examples.input: Design a visual for an internal product strategy workshop.
output: A concept direction based on systems, tension, and careful visual hierarchy.
A PNG composition with layered geometric fields, limited text, and clear workshop title placement.

output_examples.input: Make a three-page visual series about a music scene without using literal instruments.
output: A shared design philosophy for rhythm, density, and movement.
Three related PDF pages with different compositions, typography scales, and abstract reference markers.

best_practices: Specify the audience, final format, canvas size, and any required text before generation.
Use local fonts first and require approval before downloading new font files.
Ask for a final pass that checks margins, overlap, legibility, and visual hierarchy.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for imitation of a living artist or protected brand style.
Do not overload the canvas with long explanatory text.
Do not allow font downloads from unknown sources without approval.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a design philosophy and uses it to guide static PNG or PDF visual artwork.

faq.question: Does it generate the image by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides instructions for Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to use available generation or rendering tools.

faq.question: Can it make multi-page PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes guidance for multi-page visual series when the user requests them.

faq.question: Why does the audit show medium risk?
faq.answer: The skill permits downloading fonts, which creates network and supply-chain risk unless users approve trusted sources.

faq.question: Were the static high-risk findings confirmed?
faq.answer: No. The repeated high-risk matches are license text false positives, not executable malicious behavior.

faq.question: How should users run it safely?
faq.answer: Use local fonts first, approve any downloads, and review output paths before files are created.
